text:
Once upon a time in a cozy little town called Willowbrook, there was a wise old owl named Oliver. He lived high in an old oak tree at the edge of Maplewood park.

One autumn day, a snowfall swept through town. Oliver watched as the world transformed into a white dream. He couldn't see leaves anymore, just snow.

It was cold. Too cold.

A young mouse named Clara came knocking on Oliver's branch.

"I wish I could fly," she said.

Just then, a gentle wind caught her wing feathers. Off she went!

Back home, Clara made friendship bracelets.

She gave one to everyone. Everyone put theirs beside theirs.

They were beautiful.

Every year, they exchanged the most beautiful. This year was theirs.

Oliver admired their work.

"Do you think those stars mean anything?"

He chuckled.

"They're just stars. But those are some beautiful stars."

They talked dreams.

Oliver's favorite was a beautiful sparrow.

One spring, they saw that
